title_original: Neutral-Atom Quantum Computing Market 2026-2036: QuEra-Google, Atom Computing-Microsoft, and Pasqal Drive 1,000-to-Million-Qubit Roadmap Toward Fault-Tolerant Systems
summary_original: A new report from ResearchAndMarkets.com provides a 2026-2036 forecast for neutral-atom quantum computing, highlighting rapid advancements and aggressive scaling trajectories. Key players include QuEra Computing, partnered with Google, Atom Computing with Microsoft, and Pasqal, which achieved 1,000 qubits in 2024 and targets 10,000 by 2026. Current systems feature 100-300 atom arrays with single-qubit fidelities near 99.9% and two-qubit fidelities at 99.7%. The roadmap envisions million-atom, fault-tolerant systems by 2032-2035. Applications focus on quantum simulation, optimization, and machine learning for industries like pharmaceuticals and finance. Despite hurdles in coherence and gate speeds, neutral-atom tech offers room-temperature operation, scalability, and lower infrastructure complexity compared to superconducting platforms.
